title: hieracium atratum subsp. schroeterianum
intro: Hieracium atratum subsp. schroeterianum, also known as the wood hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium atratum plant, a perennial herb native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and skin irritations. The wood hawkweed has been valued for its health benefits, including its ability to support kidney function, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
